title: Executive Order 9665—Transfer of the Functions of the Smaller War Plants Corporation to the Reconstruction Finance Corporation and the Department of Commerce

flags.justification: A well-defined split of functions between two recipient bodies, specific statutory sections to RFC and the remainder to Commerce.
Sec. 3 requires both recipient bodies to continue submitting statutorily required reports.
Multiple related provisions (transfer, personnel, reporting, further-measures authority) all coherently serve one purpose: agency dissolution and functional transfer.
An agency-dissolution and transfer action, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A dissolution and transfer action, not a temporary-authority grant.
Sec. 5 explicitly preserves all prior regulations, rulings, and directives except as they conflict with this order.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A routine wartime-agency wind-down and transfer, similar to EO 9256 and EO 9577.
A careful, statute-anchored division of functions between the RFC and Commerce that avoids overlap.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
